info.name: AssetFlow API
info.description:
Enterprise Asset & Resource Management System

**Setup before testing:**
1. In Postman → Settings → enable "Automatically follow redirects" and cookie management
2. Run **Sign In** — the session cookie is stored automatically
3. `assetId`, `allocationId`, `transferId` are auto-set after each Create request via Tests scripts
4. Fill in `employeeId` and `attachmentId` manually after fetching them from list endpoints

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Q3 2026 Engineering Department Audit",
  "description": "Quarterly asset verification for all engineering assets",
  "scopeType": "DEPARTMENT",
  "departmentId": "{{departmentId}}",
  "startDate": "2026-09-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "endDate": "2026-09-30T23:59:59.000Z"
}

request.description:
scopeType: ORGANIZATION | DEPARTMENT | LOCATION

- DEPARTMENT requires departmentId
- LOCATION requires location string
- ORGANIZATION needs neither

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Annual Full Inventory Audit 2026",
  "description": "Complete organisation-wide asset verification",
  "scopeType": "ORGANIZATION",
  "startDate": "2026-12-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "endDate": "2026-12-31T23:59:59.000Z"
}

request.description: Returns audit cycle details, assigned auditors, and verification stats (total/verified/missing/damaged/unverified).

Allowed status transitions:
- PLANNED → ACTIVE
- PLANNED / ACTIVE → CANCELLED

Cannot update dates once ACTIVE.
Replaces the full auditor list. Only assigned auditors can verify assets.

verificationStatus: VERIFIED | MISSING | DAMAGED

Caller must be an assigned auditor. Audit must be ACTIVE.
Returns all MISSING and DAMAGED records with asset details, plus overall verification stats.

ADMIN only. Audit must be ACTIVE.

On close:
- MISSING assets → status set to LOST
- DAMAGED assets → flagged for review in asset history
- VERIFIED assets → no change
- All records locked (no further verification allowed)

item.name: 🏠 Dashboard
item.description:
Role-scoped dashboard endpoints. All roles can access these — data is automatically filtered by your role:
- ADMIN / ASSET_MANAGER → org-wide data
- DEPARTMENT_HEAD → their department only
- EMPLOYEE → personal data only

Log in first to establish a session.

request.description:
Returns all dashboard sections in a single request: KPIs, quick actions, recent activity, returns, booking summary, maintenance summary, and audit summary.

All sections run in parallel server-side.

Returns 14 KPI counters:
- assetsAvailable, assetsAllocated, assetsUnderMaintenance, assetsReserved, assetsLost, assetsRetired
- activeBookings, todaysBookings
- pendingTransferRequests, pendingMaintenanceRequests
- upcomingReturns (due within 7 days), overdueReturns
- activeAuditCycles, completedAuditCycles

EMPLOYEE: asset counts reflect their own allocations; transfer/audit KPIs are 0.

Latest 20 asset history entries.

Includes: timestamp, action, asset tag/name, performer name, metadata.

- ADMIN / ASSET_MANAGER → org-wide history
- DEPARTMENT_HEAD → history for assets in their department
- EMPLOYEE → history entries they personally performed

Returns two arrays:

**overdue** — allocations with status OVERDUE. Includes daysOverdue (integer).
**upcoming** — active allocations with expectedReturnDate within the next 7 days.

Both include: asset tag/name, employee name, department name, expectedReturnDate.

Returns three arrays (up to 20 each):

**today** — bookings with startTime on today's date
**ongoing** — bookings with status ONGOING
**upcoming** — bookings with status UPCOMING

Each item includes: asset name/tag, booker name, startTime, endTime, title, purpose.

Returns counts grouped by status:
- pending (PENDING)
- approved (APPROVED + TECHNICIAN_ASSIGNED combined)
- inProgress (IN_PROGRESS)
- resolved (RESOLVED)
- rejected (REJECTED)

DEPARTMENT_HEAD: scoped to assets in their department.
EMPLOYEE: scoped to requests they raised.

Returns audit cycle counts and discrepancy total:
- planned, active, completed (audit cycle status counts)
- discrepancies (total audit records with verificationStatus MISSING or DAMAGED)

EMPLOYEE: returns all zeros (no audit visibility).
DEPARTMENT_HEAD: scoped to DEPARTMENT-type cycles for their department.

item.name: 📈 Reports
item.description:
Reports & Analytics endpoints. EMPLOYEE has no access — only ADMIN, ASSET_MANAGER, and DEPARTMENT_HEAD.

DEPARTMENT_HEAD sees only data scoped to their department (departmentId is auto-injected).

All JSON endpoints accept common filters as query params:
- dateFrom / dateTo (ISO date strings)
- departmentId, categoryId, assetId, employeeId, location
- page (default 1), limit (default 50, max 500)

The export endpoint adds: report (required) and format=pdf|xlsx|csv.

request.description:
Asset utilization summary:

**summary** — total, utilized (ALLOCATED + RESERVED + UNDER_MAINTENANCE), idle (AVAILABLE), utilizationPercentage
**mostUsed** — top 10 assets by allocation count
**leastUsed** — bottom 10 assets by allocation count

Maintenance request analytics:

**summary** — total, pending, approved (APPROVED + TECHNICIAN_ASSIGNED), inProgress, resolved, rejected
**byAsset** — request counts per asset
**byCategory** — request counts per category
**byDepartment** — request counts per department
**dueForMaintenance** — upcoming (PENDING/APPROVED/TECHNICIAN_ASSIGNED, up to 50), overdue (IN_PROGRESS > 14 days), recentlyServiced (RESOLVED in last 30 days)

Asset lifecycle distribution:

**total** — total asset count
**byStatus** — array of { status, count, percentage } for every asset status (AVAILABLE, ALLOCATED, RESERVED, UNDER_MAINTENANCE, LOST, RETIRED)

Department allocation summary:

**departments** — array of { departmentId, departmentName, totalAssets, allocatedAssets, availableAssets, bookableAssets, employeeCount } for every department.

DEPARTMENT_HEAD: returns only their own department.

Booking analytics:

**summary** — total, completed, cancelled, ongoing, upcoming, completionRate (%), cancellationRate (%)
**perDay** — daily booking counts (date string + count)
**peakHours** — hourly booking counts sorted by busiest (label: "HH:00–HH:00")
**mostBooked** — top 10 bookable assets by booking count
**leastBooked** — bottom 10 bookable assets (includes 0-booking assets)

Audit cycle analytics:

**summary** — total, planned, active, completed, cancelled, completionRate (%)
**discrepancies** — missing, damaged, resolvedDiscrepancies counts across all audit records
**recentCycles** — last 10 audit cycles (name, status, scopeType, startDate, endDate)

query.description: One of: utilization, maintenance, lifecycle, bookings, departments, audits

request.description:
Download any report as a file. Returns a binary attachment.

**Required params:**
- report: utilization | maintenance | lifecycle | bookings | departments | audits
- format: pdf | xlsx | csv

All report filter params (dateFrom, dateTo, departmentId, etc.) are also accepted.

In Postman: click **Send and Download** to save the file.

Download maintenance report as Excel (.xlsx). Use **Send and Download** in Postman.

Workbook has a single sheet with multiple sections (summary, by asset, by category, by department, upcoming, overdue, recently serviced).

Header rows have a blue fill; columns are auto-sized.
Download bookings report as CSV. Sections are separated by a blank line, each preceded by a title row.

item.name: 🔔 Notifications
item.description:
Notification endpoints. All roles can access their own notifications. ADMIN can see all notifications.

Filters: type, priority, isRead (true/false), dateFrom, dateTo, page, limit.

query.description: ASSET_ASSIGNED | ASSET_RETURNED | TRANSFER_REQUEST | TRANSFER_APPROVED | TRANSFER_REJECTED | BOOKING_CREATED | BOOKING_CANCELLED | BOOKING_REMINDER | BOOKING_COMPLETED | MAINTENANCE_REQUEST | MAINTENANCE_APPROVED | MAINTENANCE_REJECTED | MAINTENANCE_COMPLETED | AUDIT_CREATED | AUDIT_ASSIGNED | AUDIT_COMPLETED | AUDIT_DISCREPANCY | OVERDUE_RETURN | SYSTEM

request.description:
Returns paginated notifications. ADMIN sees all; other roles see only their own.

Each item includes: id, userId, title, message, type, priority, referenceType, referenceId, isRead, readAt, createdAt.
Returns { count: number } — total unread notifications for the current user (or org-wide for ADMIN).
Returns a single notification by ID. Non-ADMIN users can only fetch their own notifications.
Marks a single notification as read and sets readAt to now. Returns 404 if already read or not found.
Marks all unread notifications as read for the current user.
Permanently deletes a notification. Non-ADMIN users can only delete their own.

Returns all allocations with status OVERDUE.

Each item: allocationId, assetId, assetTag, assetName, employeeId, employeeName, expectedReturnDate, allocatedAt.

EMPLOYEE: only their own overdue allocations.
ADMIN / ASSET_MANAGER / DEPT_HEAD: all overdue allocations.

Returns UPCOMING bookings that start within the next 30 minutes.

Each item: bookingId, title, purpose, assetTag, assetName, bookerName, startTime, endTime.

EMPLOYEE / DEPT_HEAD: only their own bookings.
ADMIN / ASSET_MANAGER: all upcoming bookings across the org.

item.name: 📋 Activity Logs
item.description:
Activity log endpoints. All roles can access logs but with different scopes:
- ADMIN / ASSET_MANAGER / DEPT_HEAD → org-wide (can filter by userId)
- EMPLOYEE → only their own actions

Filters: module, userId, entityType, entityId, action, search, dateFrom, dateTo, page, limit.

query.description: ASSETS | ALLOCATIONS | BOOKINGS | MAINTENANCE | AUDITS | ORGANIZATION | AUTH | SYSTEM

request.description:
Returns paginated activity logs ordered by most recent first.

Each item: id, userId, userName, userEmail, action, module, entityType, entityId, description, metadata, ipAddress, userAgent, createdAt.
Returns a single activity log entry. EMPLOYEE gets a 403 if the log belongs to another user.
Example: fetch only asset-related logs. Change module to ALLOCATIONS, BOOKINGS, MAINTENANCE, AUDITS, ORGANIZATION, AUTH, or SYSTEM.
Full-text search across description, action, and entityType fields (case-insensitive partial match).
